subject: Project Management
title: The Apollo Program
description: A fast breakdown of The Apollo Program for builders.

hook.text: NASA's Apollo program put humans on the Moon using a strictly sequential, waterfall-style approach.

buildup.text: Requirements were locked: land on the Moon and return safely. Design, build, test — each phase was exhaustive.

discovery.text: With lives at stake and no second chances, extensive upfront planning wasn't optional — it was survival.

twist.text: Even Apollo had iteration: the program ran Mercury and Gemini first as learning missions before attempting the Moon.

climax.text: Apollo proves waterfall works when requirements are truly fixed and the cost of failure is catastrophic.

punchline.text: When the requirements are literally rocket science, plan first.

quiz.question: Why did waterfall-style planning work for the Apollo program?
options: Requirements were fixed and failure was life-threatening
The project had unlimited budget and no deadlines
